name: Alto de la Sierra del Fraile y trialeras de la Canyada — Circular desde Villena
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villena offers diverse landscapes, characterized by significant mountain ranges such as Sierra de Salinas and Sierra de la Villa, alongside the Vinalopó Valley. The region features varied terrain, including holm-oak groves, pine forests, and rocky trails. These natural features provide a range of routes suitable for different skill levels, from gentle paths to challenging ascents. Best mountain bike trails around Villena The most popular mountain bike trail is Alto de la Sierra del Fraile y trialeras de la Canyada — Circular desde Villena, a 25.3 miles (40.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain through pine forests and holm-oak groves. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Colonia de Santa Eulalia – El Plano loop from Villena, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route offers scenic views across the Vinalopó Valley. Local mountain bikers also love the Santa Maria Church – Biar Forest Trail Start loop from Villena, a 32.9 miles (52.9 km) trail leading through pine forests and mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Villena is defined by mountain ranges, pine forests, and valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Villena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villena?
A: Villena offers a wide variety of mountain biking experiences, with over 110 trails listed on komoot. These routes cater to all skill levels, including 10 easy, 69 moderate, and 32 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villena?
A: The terrain around Villena is incredibly diverse, ranging from holm-oak groves and pine forests in the Sierra de Salinas and Sierra de la Villa to rocky trails and peaceful valleys. You'll also find unique landscapes like the inland salt flats (Saleros) and views across the Vinalopó Valley.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Villena?
A: Yes, Villena has trails suitable for families and beginners. While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly tagged, the region offers 10 easy-graded routes that are generally less demanding and can be enjoyed by those new to mountain biking or riding with children.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes in the Villena area?
A: For those looking for longer rides, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31508362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colonia de Santa Eulalia – El Plano loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 53.7 km. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31759464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria Church – Biar Forest Trail Start loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 52.9 km long and features significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villena?
A: Villena experiences a continental climate with hot summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Winter can also be suitable, but be prepared for colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Villena?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Villena are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930127161/alto-de-la-sierra-del-fraile-y-trialeras-de-la-canyada-circular-desde-villena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Sierra del Fraile y trialeras de la Canyada — Circular desde Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular 40.6 km loop.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Villena?
A: The diverse landscapes of Villena, particularly in areas like the Sierra de Salinas, are home to various fauna. You might spot common genets, wild cats, and wild boars, especially in the holm-oak groves and pine forests. The region's unique flora, including species adapted to challenging climatic conditions, also adds to the natural experience.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions visible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer views of historical sites. For instance, you can often see the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Castle&lt;/a&gt; (also known as Villena Castle) which offers panoramic views. The ruins of Salvatierra Castle are also visible from some trails. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4981292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María&lt;/a&gt; is a notable landmark in the town itself.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Villena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views across the Vinalopó Valley, and the opportunities to explore pine forests and mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Villena offers 32 difficult-graded routes for experienced mountain bikers. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. An example of a challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10350059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;COSS60 Bar – Calderones Pond loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial climbing.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through different towns or villages?
A: Many routes start from or pass near Villena, allowing you to explore the town. Some trails might also lead towards other nearby settlements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria Church – Villena loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt; offers a route that integrates the urban and natural landscapes.

Q: What are some notable natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Beyond the mountain ranges, keep an eye out for the unique 'Saleros' (inland salt flats) which are a distinct natural element of the landscape. The Vinalopó river also forms the 'backbone' of the area, contributing to varied scenery. You'll also find interesting Iberian gypsiferous vegetation and scrubland in areas like Los Cabezos.
tips: Beautiful views from up here towards the Sierra del Maigmó and the valleys of the Valencian Community and Castilla La Mancha.

waypoints.name: Alto de la Sierra del Fraile
